steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize the significance of a similarity between two kits. The notes show Pbact alone achieved both targets: high yield and purity close to the ideal of 1.8. CTAB 2X and Pplant both fell short of Pbact — that shared shortfall is the meaningful similarity to highlight.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — CTAB 2X and Pplant both had a DNA yield above 10 and a purity ratio above 1.4.: describes a trivial overlap, not what makes the similarity significant; also may not accurately reflect the data. ✗
  • Choice B — Due to their insufficient yield or purity, CTAB 2X and Pplant were deemed by the researchers to be less effective than Pbact.: captures the meaningful shared status — both kits failed to match Pbact on at least one key metric. ✓
  • Choice C — With the ideal purity ratio being 1.8, CTAB 2X and Pbact were equal in purity, according to the researchers.: misstates the data (CTAB 2X purity was 2.2, Pbact purity was 1.6 — not equal). ✗
  • Choice D — Compared to CTAB 2X, which had a DNA yield of 2,134 ng/μL, both Pbact and Pplant had insufficient yields; Pplant, in particular, was ineffective due to its low yield.: highlights only CTAB 2X's yield advantage (2,134 vs. lower figures), not a shared trait between two kits. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
